news.summary: Columbus Circle Capital Corp I announced the pricing of their upsized initial public offering of 22,000,000 units at $10.00 per unit. Each unit includes one Class A ordinary share and one-half of a redeemable warrant, with a whole warrant allowing the holder to purchase one share at $11.50. The units are set to be listed on Nasdaq under the symbol "CCCMU" starting May 16, 2025. The underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 3,300,000 units. The anticipated closing date for the offering is around May 19, 2025.
news.headline: Columbus Circle Capital Corp I prices $220M IPO of 22M units at $10 each, listed on Nasdaq as "CCCMU" on May 16.

news.summary: A new weekly show named "Ceasefire" will be launched on a popular public affairs channel after a hiatus of two decades. The key concern is whether members of the Republican and Democratic parties will participate in the show.
news.headline: C-SPAN set to launch “Ceasefire,” its first new weekly show in 20 years. Will it promote civil discourse between Democrats and Republicans?

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Innovent Biologics, Inc., a leading biopharmaceutical company based in Hong Kong (HKEX: 01801), announced its operations in San Francisco and Suzhou, China. The company specializes in developing, manufacturing, and distributing top-notch medications for oncologic, autoimmune, cardiovascular, and other medical conditions.
news.headline: Second Phase 3 study of Mazdutide vs Semaglutide in China completes first participant dosing for adults with overweight or obesity and fatty liver disease.

news.summary: Boehringer Ingelheim Resort, Inc., a pharmaceutical company, is reportedly accused of not properly documenting the total hours worked by its employees. This alleged negligence has led to discrepancies in the payment and official wage records of the employees. The incident took place in ALAMEDA, California on May 15, 2025. The company is facing allegations of providing inaccurate and inadequate wage statements to its workforce.
news.headline: Lawsuit filed against Boehringer Ingelheim Resort, Inc. for unpaid overtime wages and inaccurate wage recording.

news.summary: The State of Wisconsin Investment Board (SWIB) has sold all 6,060,351 shares ($355.6 million) in BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF in Q1, with no Bitcoin ETF positions reported in their 13F filing. SWIB initially bought $164 million worth of Bitcoin ETFs in Q1 2024. The Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala purchased an additional 491,439 IBIT shares in Q1, bringing their total to 8,726,972 ($512 million). IBIT surpassed $45 billion in net inflows by May 14, with a 20-day streak of inflows ending on May 13. Other Bitcoin ETFs trail IBIT in net inflows, with Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund ($11.6 billion) and ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF ($2.7 billion) being notable ones.
news.headline: Wisconsin Investment Board liquidates 6.1M IBIT shares, worth $355.6M. SWIB previously held $164M BTC in Q1 2024. Abu Dhabi's Mubadala buys 491.4K IBIT shares worth $512M. IBIT sees $45B net inflows, 20-day streak broken. FBTC, ARK trail IBIT in net inflows.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, in Toronto, Eric Trump shared his vision for digital assets and introduced his latest venture, American Bitcoin, a bitcoin mining company established in collaboration with Hut 8. Eric Trump's appearance at Consensus 2025 drew significant attention, with a focus on his plans for the future. The company's partnership with Hut 8 and its commitment to the world of cryptocurrency signify a new direction for Eric Trump, aligning with the current trends in the industry. This move demonstrates a significant step towards further establishing Eric Trump's presence in the digital assets market.
news.headline: Eric Trump announces plans for American Bitcoin at Consensus 2025 with mining firm Hut 8. Main attraction at event on May 15, 2025.

news.summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited has disclosed the pricing details of a recent private offering. The offering comprises US$500 million of senior notes due 2035 at an issue price of 99.632% and US$400 million of senior notes due 2055 at an issue price of 99.725%. These notes, collectively referred to as the "Notes," represent unsecured senior obligations of Fairfax. The 2035 Notes will bear interest at a fixed rate of 5.750% per annum, while the 2055 Notes will bear interest at a fixed rate of 6.500% per annum.
news.headline: Fairfax prices $500M 2035 Notes at 99.632% and $400M 2055 Notes at 99.725%. 2035 Notes - 5.750% interest, 2055 Notes - 6.500%.

news.summary: In Merrill, Wisconsin on May 15, 2025, Thorogood Workwear, the authorized licensee and retail collaborator of Thorogood® by Weinbrenner Shoe Company, introduced its latest USA-Made Workwear Collection. This new line can be pre-ordered starting on May 21, 2025. Stay tuned for more updates on this exciting launch.
news.headline: Thorogood Workwear introduces USA-made collection and limited-edition boots ahead of Memorial Day, pre-orders start May 21, 2025.

news.summary: Starting on May 30, the upcoming round of payouts to FTX creditors will commence, totaling more than $5 billion in recovered assets to be distributed.
news.headline: FTX to distribute $5B+ in bankruptcy repayments to creditors starting May 30, with recovered funds being sent out.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Red White & Bloom Brands Inc. (CSE: RWB) released an update regarding a management cease trade order (MCTO) issued on May 1, 2025, by the British Columbia Securities Commission under National Policy 12-203. The MCTO was put in place in relation to the Company, and it affects the trading activities of RWB until certain filing requirements are met. This order prevents the Chief Executive Officer and the Chief Financial Officer from trading in securities. The MCTO will be lifted once the necessary filings are submitted, and shareholders informed accordingly. This order is intended to protect investors by ensuring a transparent and thorough disclosure of relevant information.
news.headline: Red White & Bloom Brands gives update on management cease trade order issued by BC Securities Commission.

news.summary: Kohl’s Corporation has confirmed the pricing of its private offering of $360 million aggregate principal amount of 10.000% senior secured notes due 2030. The notes, guaranteed by Kohl’s subsidiaries, will be secured by 11 distribution centers and e-commerce fulfillment facilities. The offering aims to raise funds and enhance financial security, demonstrating Kohl’s proactive approach to financial management and growth.
news.headline: Kohl's Corp priced $360M senior secured notes due 2030 in private offering, guaranteed by subsidiaries.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Bar None Auction launched its third site in the Western United States in Fontana, California. This new addition joined the existing locations in Sacramento, California, and Woodburn, Oregon, which were established in October 2024. Bar None Auction has expanded its operations to cater to the growing demand in the region. The Fontana site is fully operational, with a dedicated team trained to manage auctions seamlessly. The incorporation of advanced IT systems has further streamlined processes, enhancing efficiency for both the company and its customers.
news.headline: Bar None Auction opens 3rd location in Fontana, CA in Oct 2024, following sites in Sacramento, CA and Woodburn, OR.

news.summary: Ontario, California, on May 15, 2025 - The City of Ontario has dismissed the contentious claims made by the Inland Empire Utilities Agency (IEUA) in a public letter dated May 9, 2025. The city believes that IEUA's suggestions of cooperation are insincere and designed to deceive, despite...
news.headline: Ontario criticizes IEUA for blame game in letter received on May 9, 2025, citing lack of collaboration.

news.summary: Karnalyte Resources Inc. announced their financial results for the first quarter of 2025, including the filing of restated financial statements for the previous year. During Q1 2025, the company focused on advancing their NI 43-101 compliant technical report for mine development. They reviewed and refined technical sections, updated price forecasts, and aligned strategies with market conditions. The company is committed to advancing their potash project with a robust offtake agreement. They also evaluated the economic potential of increasing magnesium chloride production alongside the Potash Project using advanced mining technologies. Karnalyte listed and sold non-core farmland parcels in March 2025, generating net proceeds of $1,409,000. Their outlook for 2025 includes completing the technical report update, optimizing construction plans, enhancing project sustainability, and reviewing their development strategy. They aim to intensify business development activities, seek partnerships, and drive sustainable growth.
news.headline: Karnalyte Resources Inc. reported Q1 2025 results, restated 2024 financials, and progress on its NI 43-101 technical report. The company listed three farmland parcels for sale, closing in April with $1,409,000 net proceeds. Plans for 2025 include completing the technical report update, optimizing construction plans, and reviewing the development strategy. Karnalyte aims to enhance project sustainability, optimize asset portfolio, and seek partnerships for project advancement.

news.summary: In the second quarter of 2025, a quarterly distribution of C$0.02 per common share was announced, marking the twenty-third consecutive quarter of shareholder distributions.
news.headline: Mount Logan Capital Inc. declares C$0.02 per share distribution for Q2 2025, marking 23 quarters of consecutive distributions.

news.summary: White Castle recently announced the induction of 14 individuals into its prestigious hall of fame. Among the honorees are longtime friends who have been organizing an annual Slider-eating contest since 1967, a dedicated woman who cycled 36 miles to visit a White Castle location, and a renowned glam rocker who penned a song centered around eating Sliders. The induction ceremony took place in Columbus, Ohio on May 15, 2025. This event marked a significant milestone for White Castle and honored individuals who have demonstrated exceptional dedication and creativity in their relationship with the iconic fast-food chain.
news.headline: White Castle inducts 14 devoted fans into Cravers Hall of Fame. Includes those part of annual contest since 1967 and woman who biked 36 miles to get there.
Globalwafers plans to expand US investment by $4 billion to $7.5 billion

news.summary: InventHelp's Virtual Invention Browsing Experience, VIBE, is scheduled to showcase at the prestigious Licensing Expo 2025 in Las Vegas, Nevada. Known as the biggest licensing trade show worldwide, the event is set to welcome visitors from May 20 to May 22. This expo serves as a key platform for innovators and businesses to explore licensing opportunities, expand their networks, and forge partnerships.
news.headline: InventHelp®'s VIBE to showcase at Licensing Expo 2025, the world's biggest licensing trade show in Las Vegas May 20-22.
